targets.label: NPC / 角色
targets.description: Character data entries used for NPC definitions.
targets.key_hint: NPC internal name, e.g. Abigail or ExampleNPC.

fields.name: CanSocialize
fields.notes: Wiki 优先：可用 true/false 或游戏状态查询表达式控制是否可社交。

fields.name: CanVisitIsland
fields.notes: Wiki 优先字段。旧工程中可能出现 CanVisitIslandCondition，本工具会尽量兼容读取。

fields.name: UnlockConditions
fields.notes: 角色解锁条件；留空表示不额外限制。

fields.name: WinterStarParticipant
fields.notes: 是否参与冬星盛宴，可填 true/false 或游戏状态查询。

creation_checklist: Add Data/NPCGiftTastes entry if the NPC can receive gifts.
Add Characters/schedules/<NPC> schedule asset if they move around.
Provide portrait and overworld sprite textures. Use SVE-style folders such as assets/CharacterFiles/Portraits/<NPC>/<file>.png and assets/CharacterFiles/OverworldSprites/<NPC>/<file>.png.
Load portraits to Portraits/<NPC>. Load custom sprite sheets to a stable Mods/<UniqueID>/... asset path and reference that path from Data/Characters.TextureName.
Use unique internal NPC IDs for custom NPCs to avoid collisions.
ai_guidance: For a new social NPC, generate a bundle of Data/Characters, gift tastes, dialogue, schedule, and sprite/portrait asset requirements.
Use enum values exactly, such as Female/Male/Undefined and Child/Teen/Adult.
Keep character assets grouped under assets/CharacterFiles/Portraits, assets/CharacterFiles/OverworldSprites, assets/CharacterFiles/Dialogue, and assets/CharacterFiles/Schedules.
For roommate routes, create a Data/Objects proposal item with ContextTags containing propose_roommate_<npc name>, and Load its texture to the Texture target used by that item.
Do not assume romance or gifting is enabled unless the user asks for it.

targets.label: 物品 / 对象
targets.key_hint: Qualified or internal item ID depending on target data format.

item_data_targets.description: Fruit tree growth and product data.

item_id_guidance: Prefer qualified item IDs like (O)388, (BC)12, or custom qualified IDs when referencing items.
Use stable custom item IDs and avoid numeric IDs for new items unless matching existing vanilla content.
When adding gift tastes, shop items, mail attachments, or spawn rules, use the same item ID consistently.
ai_guidance: Choose the correct item data target before generating JSON; do not put all item types in Data/Objects.
Use Category and Edibility enums when editing basic objects.
For player-facing item names/descriptions, prefer i18n keys if translations are enabled.

targets.label: 对话
targets.description: Dialogue data for a character-specific dialogue asset.
targets.key_hint: Dialogue key such as Mon, Tue, rainy, fall_15, eventSeen_123, or custom keys.

fields.name: value
fields.label: 对话文本
fields.notes: May include @ for player name, #$b# line breaks, portrait/emote commands, and response blocks.

key_patterns.description: Default dialogue for a day of week.
Dialogue for a specific calendar date.
Dialogue after the player has seen an event.
Custom keys can be referenced by game data, events, or CP edits.

formats.label: <dayOfMonth>
formats.warning: Wiki: this key is first-year-only; usually prefer <dayOfMonth>_*.

formats.label: <dayOfWeek><hearts>_<firstOrLaterYear>
formats.notes: UI 提供 1-14 心；Wiki 说明原版通用好感 key 通常按 10/8/6/4/2 检查，使用其它数值时请确认目标版本或模组逻辑。

formats.label: <dayOfWeek><hearts>
formats.notes: UI 提供 1-14 心；Wiki 说明原版通用好感 key 通常按 10/8/6/4/2 检查，使用其它数值时请确认目标版本或模组逻辑。

formats.label: <season>_<dayOfWeek><hearts>
formats.notes: UI 提供 1-14 心；Wiki 说明原版通用好感 key 通常按 10/8/6/4/2 检查，使用其它数值时请确认目标版本或模组逻辑。

formats.label: Wiki 婚后/室友真实 Key
formats.notes: 展开 Wiki 中 <random> 的真实 key；不使用 xxx_n 占位。

ai_guidance: Return dialogue suggestions as {target, key, i18n_key, text, category, fields}.
Use {{i18n:key}} in Entries and put the actual line in i18n/default.json.
Dialogue key conditions are not CP When conditions; choose a dialogue key format first.

commands.description: End response block / jump marker in response dialogue.
Reveal a gift taste in supported dialogue contexts.
Give an item in supported dialogue contexts.
Run a trigger action in supported dialogue contexts.

ai_guidance: For new NPC dialogue, initialize Characters/Dialogue/<NPC> with a Load patch from assets/blank.json, then apply EditData entries.
For romance or roommate dialogue, also initialize Characters/Dialogue/MarriageDialogue<NPC> with assets/blank.json.
Use i18n references in Entries, e.g. {{i18n:Sophia.CharacterDialogue.spring-Mon}}, and place the actual line in i18n/default.json.
When generating AI dialogue suggestions, return { target, key, i18n_key, text } rather than putting long text directly in Entries.
Use @ for the player name instead of inventing a player token inside dialogue strings.
Use #$b# for a new dialogue box rather than literal newlines unless editing JSON makes that clearer.
Keep dialogue keys stable and avoid overwriting vanilla keys unless the user explicitly wants a replacement.

targets.label: 商店
targets.description: Shop definitions and inventory. Each entry key is a shop ID, and the value contains shop settings plus an Items array.
targets.key_hint: Unique shop ID, e.g. SeedShop, FishShop, AnimalShop, Saloon, IslandShop, QiGemShop, or a custom shop ID.

item_fields.name: Id
item_fields.description: Unique ID for this shop item entry within the shop.

item_fields.name: ItemId
item_fields.description: Item to sell, preferably a qualified item ID like (O)388 or a custom qualified ID.

item_fields.name: AvailableStock
item_fields.description: Available stock count. Use -1 for unlimited stock in common cases.

item_fields.name: AvailableStockLimit
item_fields.description: Maximum restockable stock limit when separate from current available stock.

item_fields.name: Condition
item_fields.description: Game state query controlling whether the item is sold.

item_fields.name: ActionsOnPurchase
item_fields.description: Trigger actions to run after purchase.

substructures.name: Items
substructures.description: Array of shop item models. Minimal custom item rows usually need Id, ItemId, and Price.

substructures.name: Owners
substructures.description: Optional owner definitions used by some shops and shop menus.

ai_guidance: Target Data/Shops and use the shop ID as the entry key.
Use Items as an array of shop item models with Id, ItemId, Price, and optional AvailableStock, AvailableStockLimit, Stack, Condition, and ActionsOnPurchase.
For Pierre, use SeedShop rather than Pierre as the shop key.
When adding a custom item to an existing shop, preserve existing shop fields and patch only the Items data needed by the mod.
Use Condition for date, friendship, mail, event, or config-gated stock instead of hardcoding separate duplicate shop entries.

targets.label: 事件
targets.key_hint: Event ID plus slash-separated preconditions, e.g. 999999/f Abigail 2500.

fields.name: value
fields.label: 事件脚本
fields.notes: Slash-separated commands; supports comments and dialogue commands.

key_patterns.description: Always use a unique numeric event ID.

preconditions.description: Day requirement in supported event key contexts.

ai_guidance: Event scripts are slash-separated command strings; quote spoken text when needed.
Use a large unique event ID for custom events and avoid colliding with vanilla event IDs.
For simple date/weather gating in CP, prefer When conditions outside the event if possible.

targets.label: 礼物喜好
targets.description: Universal and NPC-specific gift taste definitions.
targets.key_hint: NPC name, or universal keys such as Universal_Love / Universal_Like.
targets.recommended_patch: TextOperations can be safer than replacing a full NPC gift taste string when appending items.

keys.description: Items loved by most NPCs unless overridden.
Items liked by most NPCs unless overridden.
Items treated neutrally by most NPCs unless overridden.
Items disliked by most NPCs unless overridden.
Items hated by most NPCs unless overridden.

text_operation_templates.notes: Use delimiter-aware operations or a focused field edit when adding items to an existing legacy taste string.
Only replace a full entry when the user intentionally owns the whole taste definition.

ai_examples.description: Add a custom item to Abigail love list using a focused patch concept.

ai_guidance: Prefer TextOperations or narrow edits when appending gift tastes, because many mods edit the same entries.
Use qualified item IDs like (O)388 when the item type matters.
Do not reorder or replace all universal tastes unless the user explicitly asks.

targets.label: 日程
targets.description: NPC schedule assets and daily schedule entries.
targets.key_hint: Schedule key such as spring, summer_15, rain, marriage_Mon, or custom condition keys.

fields.name: value
fields.label: 日程脚本
fields.notes: Usually time map x y direction segments, optionally with dialogue or commands.

key_patterns.description: Schedule for a specific season day.

schedule_points.description: Facing direction, commonly 0 up, 1 right, 2 down, 3 left.
Optional extra animation, behavior, or dialogue depending on context.

ai_guidance: Build schedules as slash-separated time/location points.
Keep map names and tile coordinates explicit; do not invent coordinates if the user did not provide them.
Use CP When conditions or schedule keys for broad timing rules depending on the user goal.

targets.label: 邮件
targets.description: Mail message entries with optional attachments, recipes, money, or actions.

fields.name: value
fields.label: 邮件正文
fields.notes: May include message text, item attachments, recipe unlocks, money, and title markers.

mail_markers.description: Attach or grant an item in supported mail syntax.
Unlock a recipe in supported mail syntax.
Run a trigger action from mail in supported contexts.
Title marker / title separator in mail formats where supported.
Line or paragraph separator in mail text where supported.

attachment_types.description: Give one or more items to the player.
Unlock a cooking or crafting recipe.
Give money when supported by the mail format.

ai_guidance: Use a unique mail key with an author/mod prefix for custom mail.
Prefer i18n keys for mail title/body when the pack has translations.
If a mail reward needs logic, create a TriggerAction and call it from the mail instead of packing complex behavior into the text.

targets.label: 触发动作
targets.description: Trigger action definitions which can run actions from dialogue, events, mail, or other triggers.

field_details.name: Id
field_details.description: Stable unique ID for the trigger action.

field_details.name: Trigger
field_details.description: Trigger type or custom trigger name.

field_details.name: HostOnly
field_details.description: Whether only the host should run it in multiplayer.

field_details.name: Condition
field_details.description: Optional condition data depending on trigger/action system.

field_details.name: Repeatable
field_details.description: Whether it can run more than once when supported.

action_examples.description: Add a mail flag / send mail in supported action contexts.
Give money in supported action contexts.
Give an item in supported action contexts.
Add a quest in supported action contexts.
Mark an event as seen in supported action contexts.
Run another trigger action by ID.

trigger_examples.description: Run manually from a mail/dialogue/event action call.
Run at the start of a day.
Run when a location is entered.
Run from an event command context.

ai_guidance: Use namespaced IDs for custom trigger actions.
When actions are invoked from mail/dialogue/event text, keep the TriggerAction entry small and focused.
Set HostOnly true for rewards that should not duplicate in multiplayer unless the user says otherwise.

targets.label: 地点
targets.description: Location data entries including fish, forage, artifacts, music, and custom location settings.
targets.key_hint: Location name, e.g. Town, Forest, Mountain, or a custom location name.

substructures.name: Fish
substructures.description: Fish spawn rules, often conditioned by season, weather, time, and location context.

substructures.name: Forage
substructures.description: Forage spawn rules and item choices.

substructures.name: ArtifactSpots
substructures.description: Artifact spot spawn data and item chances.

substructures.name: CreateOnLoad
substructures.description: Create a custom location when the save loads.

creation_checklist: Provide a map asset under Maps/<LocationName> when creating a new location.
Add warps or map properties separately when needed.
Add NPC schedules/dialogue/events referencing the location only after the internal name is stable.
ai_guidance: Do not invent map asset paths or warp coordinates; ask for them or leave placeholders.
Separate Data/Locations edits from map edits; use EditMap for tile/map changes.
For fish/forage/artifact rules, keep conditions explicit and prefer arrays/objects over legacy strings when the target uses model data.

targets.label: Big craftable object
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Crop growth and harvest data.
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Fruit tree growth and product data.
targets.description: Fruit tree growth and product data.
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Weapon
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Boot
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Hat
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Shirt/clothing
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Pants/clothing
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

targets.label: Furniture
targets.key_hint: Use a stable item ID; prefer qualified IDs when referencing this item from other data.

object_categories.label: 肉类/动物产品 Animal Product (-14)
皮埃尔出售 Sell At Pierre's (-17)
鱼店出售 Sell At Fish Shop (-23)

unique_id.label: 唯一 ID
unique_id.description: A unique namespaced identifier, usually AuthorName.ModName.EntryName.

asset_name.label: 游戏资源名
asset_name.description: A game asset path such as Data/Objects, Characters/Dialogue/Abigail, or Maps/Town.

qualified_item_id.label: 限定物品 ID
qualified_item_id.description: A qualified item ID where the prefix identifies the item type.

item_query.label: 物品查询
item_query.description: A query string used by some data fields to describe matching items.

translation_key.label: 翻译键
translation_key.description: A key in i18n/default.json or locale-specific translation files.

color.label: 颜色
color.description: Named color or RGBA-style color depending on field.

duration.label: 时长
duration.description: Usually milliseconds or game-specific time depending on field.

game_state_query.label: 游戏状态查询
game_state_query.description: A condition expression evaluated by the game; used by many data fields for unlocks or behavior gates.

tokenizable_string.label: 可使用 token 的文本
tokenizable_string.description: A string where Content Patcher tokens or game tokens may be substituted depending on context.

item_spawn_fields.label: 物品生成字段
item_spawn_fields.description: Common fields used by item spawn models, usually including Id, ItemId, MinStack, MaxStack, and Condition.

ai_guidance: For game data edits, prefer EditData patches targeting the exact asset path.
Preserve raw game field names and enum values; translate labels only in UI.
When a value might depend on another mod or future game version, keep it as a custom string instead of rejecting it.
